content: We can integrate the ICM curiosity mechanism into HippoRAG2's long-term memory framework to let autonomous agents proactively prune and reinforce connections between stored knowledge based on the novelty of encountered information, turning static retrieval-augmented memory into a self-organizing, curiosity-driven knowledge graph that grows with the agent's exploration. This creates a continually learning memory system that mimics how human curiosity prioritizes updating and connecting new unexpected information in long-term memory, rather than just storing all new knowledge uniformly.
